To provide my analysis:

Isolated defects such as those involving the lenticular process of the incus are indeed quite common due to their localized nature and lower severity compared to complete fixations. However, it's important to note that while these isolated defects can be frequent, they might not necessarily encompass all cases of congenital ossicular dysfunction. There could still be other types of defects that occur but are rarer or have different presentations.

Given this context, I would slightly refine our earlier assessment by considering if there's any additional data suggesting another form of defect might also frequently present itself alongside or instead of isolated ones. Nonetheless, based on current understanding, isolated lenticular process defects remain prominent.
